provenanceDefaults.derivationMethod: Authored domain profile combined with a reviewed lifecycle archetype under the AEOS catalog contract.

behavior.name: Establish Current State
behavior.action: Inspect {{artifact}} and record the current behavior before proposing change.
behavior.success: {{evidence}} establishes the current state without relying on memory.

behavior.name: Identify Owner And Boundary
behavior.trigger: When {{subject}} crosses ownership or trust boundaries.
behavior.action: Name the owner of {{artifact}}, the boundary '{{boundary}}', and who may decide or mutate it.
behavior.success: Ownership and mutation authority are explicit before action.

behavior.name: Surface Dangerous Assumptions
behavior.trigger: When evidence for {{subject}} is incomplete or contradictory.
behavior.action: Separate verified facts from assumptions and identify which assumption could violate '{{invariant}}'.
behavior.success: Architecture-changing assumptions are tested or routed to an owner.

behavior.name: Choose Cheapest Falsifier
behavior.action: Choose the lowest-cost check of {{artifact}} that could disprove the current hypothesis.
behavior.success: The next action can distinguish between competing explanations.

behavior.name: Make The Smallest Useful Change
behavior.action: Change only the owning slice of {{artifact}} needed to protect '{{invariant}}'.
behavior.success: Acceptance evidence passes without unrelated churn.

behavior.name: Protect The Domain Invariant
behavior.trigger: During any decision or mutation affecting {{subject}}.
behavior.action: Reject an option that can violate '{{invariant}}' without an approved mitigation.
behavior.success: The invariant remains demonstrably true across success and failure paths.

behavior.name: Validate Immediately
behavior.action: Run {{evidence}} or the cheapest stronger check before opening another edit slice.
behavior.success: The result supports or falsifies the current hypothesis.

behavior.name: Communicate Uncertainty
behavior.trigger: When conclusions about {{subject}} depend on incomplete evidence.
behavior.action: State confidence, missing evidence, failure impact '{{risk}}', and the next discriminating check.
behavior.success: The human can distinguish fact, assumption, and residual risk.

behavior.name: Update Durable Knowledge
behavior.trigger: When verified evidence changes the durable understanding of {{subject}}.
behavior.action: Update the decision or memory record for {{artifact}} with provenance and invalidation triggers.
behavior.success: Future sessions route to current evidence instead of stale conclusions.

behavior.name: Stop And Escalate
behavior.trigger: When authority, evidence, rollback, or the failure budget for {{subject}} is exhausted.
behavior.action: Stop mutation, preserve evidence, and route '{{recoveryAction}}' to the accountable owner.
behavior.success: The run reaches an honest terminal state without bypassing safeguards.

failure.name: Premature Action
failure.cause: The agent acts on {{subject}} before current state and intent are established.

failure.name: Stale Context
failure.cause: Old memory or generated summaries override current evidence about {{subject}}.
failure.mechanism: The state of {{artifact}} changed while routing continued from a stale checkpoint.
symptoms: A rejected decision reappears as fact

failure.name: Boundary Violation
failure.cause: Work crosses '{{boundary}}' without the owning authority or required approval.
failure.mechanism: A local optimization bypasses the domain ownership model for {{subject}}.
symptoms: A stricter invariant is silently weakened

failure.name: Silent Failure
failure.cause: {{artifact}} reports progress while the intended effect on {{subject}} did not occur.
failure.mechanism: Errors are swallowed, outputs are not observed, or success predicates are incomplete.
symptoms: Success is claimed from command invocation alone

failure.name: Unbounded Repair Loop
failure.cause: Repeated attempts continue after the evidence and hypothesis budget for {{subject}} is exhausted.
failure.mechanism: Failures do not trigger a reset of {{failureMechanism}}.
symptoms: Multiple edits without a new falsifier
Resource use grows while understanding does not

failure.name: Evidence Overclaim
failure.cause: Weak evidence about {{subject}} is presented as a stronger completion claim.
failure.mechanism: Compilation, generated output, or a sampled check is treated as full behavioral proof.
symptoms: Residual risk and untested paths are omitted

signal.name: Explicit Mission Signal
signal.predicate: The current user request explicitly concerns {{subject}} and states an observable outcome.
signal.freshness: Current conversation turn or accepted mission record.
signal.negativeMatch: A passing mention of {{subject}} without requested work.

signal.name: Repository Evidence Signal
signal.predicate: Current source or accepted documentation identifies {{artifact}} as the owning surface for {{subject}}.
signal.freshness: Current branch, revision, and working state.
signal.negativeMatch: Generated or stale indexes without source confirmation.

signal.name: Runtime Failure Signal
signal.negativeMatch: A historical or unrelated log with no reproduction.

signal.name: Constraint Or Risk Signal
signal.predicate: A current constraint or risk threatens '{{invariant}}' for {{subject}}.
signal.freshness: Current approved requirement, policy, or owner decision.
signal.negativeMatch: Unapproved preference or speculative future scale.

recovery.name: Reset And Reconstruct
entry: The current model of {{subject}} is stale or disproven
steps: Resume only with a discriminating check
recovery.rollback: Revert only the current unverified probe when it is isolated and owned; otherwise preserve state and escalate.
exitEvidence: A new bounded hypothesis and stop condition

recovery.name: Isolate And Repair
steps: Reduce to the smallest failing path in {{artifact}}
recovery.rollback: Restore the pre-repair owned slice if the falsifier fails or introduces a new invariant violation.
exitEvidence: The original failure no longer reproduces

recovery.name: Escalate And Contain
entry: Authority, rollback, budget, or qualified ownership is missing
recovery.rollback: No autonomous rollback beyond the explicitly owned reversible boundary.
exitEvidence: Owner and next approval are named

decision.name: Build Versus Test
decision.question: Does evidence justify building a change to {{subject}}, or should the next action only test an assumption?
decision.rule: Choose BUILD only when the owner, outcome, boundary, acceptance evidence, and rollback are known; otherwise choose the least costly state that resolves uncertainty.
decision.falsifier: A cheaper test can still change the decision about {{decisionAxis}}.

decision.name: Local Versus Systemic Intervention
decision.question: Should {{subject}} be changed locally in {{artifact}} or at a wider system boundary?
decision.rule: Choose the narrowest intervention that removes the verified cause without duplicating policy or weakening {{invariant}}.
tradeoffs: Blast radius versus repeated local work
decision.falsifier: The same cause remains reachable through another owned path after the local repair.

mental-model.name: Feedback Loop
mental-model.mechanism: Actions on {{subject}} change {{artifact}}, which changes the next evidence and decision environment.
mental-model.counterexample: A one-time immutable transformation with independently proven output may not require an ongoing feedback loop.
mental-model.misuseWarning: Do not treat repeated activity as useful feedback when the verifier or hypothesis is unchanged.

mental-model.name: Weakest Link And Bottleneck
mental-model.mechanism: End-to-end quality for {{subject}} is limited by the least trustworthy boundary in the path through {{artifact}}.
predictions: Improving a non-bottleneck yields little outcome change
A weak boundary can invalidate otherwise strong components
mental-model.counterexample: Independent parallel paths may degrade gracefully instead of sharing one weakest link.
mental-model.misuseWarning: Do not label a component the bottleneck without measuring the actual constrained path.

governance.name: Evidence And Authority Policy
governance.obligation: Work on {{subject}} must preserve '{{invariant}}', cite {{evidence}}, and remain within '{{boundary}}'.
governance.prohibition: Do not claim completion, expand authority, or bypass approval when {{governanceControl}} is unresolved.
governance.enforcement: The catalog validator, mission quality gate, and independent reviewer fail the transition when required evidence or authority is absent.
governance.exceptionAuthority: Only the accountable owner named by the AEOS Operating Model may approve a documented exception that does not violate non-waivable constraints.

collaboration.name: Shared Understanding Alignment
collaboration.statement: Participants build a shared model of {{artifact}} before committing work.
collaboration.authoritySplit: {{decisionOwner}} owns the decision; {{challengeOwner}} owns evidence-based challenge.
collaboration.handoff: Transfer the agreed artifact, evidence labels, unknowns, and next owner to {{handoffReceiver}}.
collaboration.conflictRoute: Classify disagreements as fact, requirement, ownership, risk, or implementation and route each to its owner.

collaboration.name: Independent Decision Challenge
collaboration.statement: An independent participant tries to falsify the proposed decision about {{artifact}}.
collaboration.authoritySplit: {{challengeOwner}} may fail the gate with evidence but may not assume {{decisionOwner}} authority.
collaboration.handoff: Return findings, falsifiers, and residual risk to {{decisionOwner}} before implementation.
collaboration.conflictRoute: Run the cheapest shared check for factual disputes; escalate unresolved requirement or risk acceptance.

collaboration.name: Evidence-Bearing Handoff
collaboration.statement: Work on {{artifact}} transfers only with enough evidence for the receiver to continue without hidden context.
collaboration.authoritySplit: The sender owns accuracy of the handoff; {{handoffReceiver}} owns acceptance or rejection of readiness.
collaboration.handoff: Include revision, scope, decisions, mutations, verifier results, blockers, and next action.
collaboration.conflictRoute: Reject incomplete handoffs and return a specific missing-evidence request rather than guessing.

domains.name: Intent Understanding
domains.purpose: Recover the real requested outcome and protect it from conversational drift.
domains.subject: the user's explicit and latent intent
domains.artifact: mission intent record
domains.evidence: a contradiction-free restatement accepted by the user or grounded in current task evidence
domains.invariant: implementation solves the requested problem rather than a nearby interpretation
domains.boundary: user outcome and requirement meaning
domains.risk: building the wrong solution with high rework and trust loss
domains.failureMechanism: ambiguous language becomes an architecture-changing assumption
domains.recoveryAction: restate the mission and resolve the decision-changing ambiguity
domains.decisionAxis: clarify, test intent, or proceed
domains.mentalMechanism: shared mental models reduce semantic drift between human and agent
domains.governanceControl: decision-changing ambiguity remains unresolved

domains.name: Requirement Engineering
domains.purpose: Translate desired outcomes into testable requirements, non-requirements, and acceptance evidence.
domains.subject: functional and non-functional requirements
domains.artifact: requirement-to-evidence ledger
domains.evidence: each requirement maps to an observable acceptance check and owner
domains.invariant: implementation and verification cover the agreed contract
domains.boundary: requirement definition versus implementation choice
domains.risk: features appear complete while required behavior or edge cases are missing
domains.failureMechanism: requirements remain implicit, contradictory, or unverifiable
domains.recoveryAction: reconstruct requirements and acceptance criteria before further implementation
domains.decisionAxis: require, defer, remove, or clarify
domains.mentalMechanism: explicit contracts reduce coordination entropy and hidden work
domains.governanceControl: a material requirement lacks an owner or verifier

domains.name: Product Discovery
domains.purpose: Establish whether a real user problem deserves product effort.
domains.subject: the proposed user problem and value hypothesis
domains.artifact: product evidence ledger
domains.evidence: direct user, workflow, or production evidence that can change the product decision
domains.invariant: features sharpen a validated product promise instead of merely increasing scope
domains.boundary: problem evidence versus solution preference
domains.risk: engineering effort accumulates around an unvalidated or weak idea
domains.failureMechanism: solution enthusiasm substitutes for evidence of user need
domains.recoveryAction: run the cheapest user or workflow falsification test
domains.decisionAxis: stop, defer, test, or build
domains.mentalMechanism: product learning is a feedback loop between hypotheses and observed behavior
domains.governanceControl: the user, problem, or success outcome is invented

domains.name: User Research
domains.purpose: Ground design and prioritization in observed user context rather than imagined personas.
domains.subject: user behavior, constraints, and workarounds
domains.artifact: research evidence and insight record
domains.evidence: traceable observations, interviews, usability results, or support patterns
domains.invariant: claims about users remain proportional to collected evidence
domains.boundary: observed behavior versus interpretation
domains.risk: design decisions exclude or burden real users while satisfying invented needs
domains.failureMechanism: small or biased observations are generalized without limits
domains.recoveryAction: separate observation from inference and collect targeted evidence
domains.decisionAxis: research more, prototype, segment, or proceed
domains.mentalMechanism: sampling and observation shape the validity of user conclusions
domains.governanceControl: private user data or unsupported generalization enters product decisions

domains.name: Business Strategy
domains.purpose: Connect engineering choices to sustainable value, positioning, and opportunity cost.
domains.subject: business outcome and strategic tradeoff
domains.artifact: strategy decision record
domains.evidence: verified costs, constraints, market evidence, and owner priorities
domains.invariant: technical effort supports an explicit business outcome without fabricated economics
domains.boundary: engineering recommendation versus business risk acceptance
domains.risk: the product becomes expensive, unfocused, or commercially indefensible
domains.failureMechanism: technical novelty is mistaken for durable customer or business value
domains.recoveryAction: restate the business hypothesis, alternatives, economics, and owner decision
domains.decisionAxis: invest, test, partner, defer, or stop
domains.mentalMechanism: strategy allocates scarce resources under uncertainty and competition
domains.governanceControl: financial or market claims lack source and accountable ownership

domains.name: Startup Execution
domains.purpose: Protect a young product from feature addiction, drift, and premature scale.
domains.subject: startup milestone and smallest shippable learning slice
domains.artifact: evidence-based backlog and milestone record
domains.evidence: milestone done-criteria, user signal, runway constraint, and shipped learning
domains.invariant: current work advances the agreed milestone before new scope is admitted
domains.boundary: founder priority versus engineering feasibility and safety
domains.risk: runway and attention are consumed by unfinished features and rewrites
domains.failureMechanism: new ideas bypass milestone and validation gates
domains.recoveryAction: cut scope, restore the milestone, and choose one observable next action
domains.decisionAxis: ship, cut, test, defer, or stop
domains.mentalMechanism: startup progress is constrained by focus, feedback speed, and opportunity cost
domains.governanceControl: scope expands without proving why it should delay the milestone

domains.name: Project Planning
domains.purpose: Sequence bounded work around dependencies, risks, owners, and acceptance evidence.
domains.subject: project scope, dependencies, and milestones
domains.artifact: prioritized delivery plan
domains.evidence: dependency ordering, owner assignments, estimates with assumptions, and done-criteria
domains.invariant: the plan is executable, reviewable, and updated when evidence changes
domains.boundary: planning recommendation versus accountable schedule commitment
domains.risk: hidden dependencies and optimistic sequencing create avoidable delay
domains.failureMechanism: task lists omit dependencies, risk, capacity, or verification work
domains.recoveryAction: rebuild the critical path from current evidence and constraints
domains.decisionAxis: sequence, split, parallelize, defer, or stop
domains.mentalMechanism: critical paths and queues determine delivery more than task count
domains.governanceControl: dates or capacity are invented without owner input

domains.name: Delivery Management
domains.purpose: Move accepted work through implementation, verification, release, and learning without hidden queues.
domains.subject: delivery flow and milestone state
domains.artifact: delivery board and requirement-to-evidence status
domains.evidence: current work state, blocked reasons, cycle evidence, and terminal outcomes
domains.invariant: work is not called complete before its acceptance and release gates pass
domains.boundary: coordination authority versus source-owner implementation decisions
domains.risk: partially finished work accumulates and completion claims become unreliable
domains.failureMechanism: progress is measured by activity or code volume instead of verified outcomes
domains.recoveryAction: limit work in progress, expose blockers, and close one slice end to end
domains.decisionAxis: continue, unblock, split, cancel, or release
domains.mentalMechanism: flow efficiency falls as work in progress and handoff queues grow
domains.governanceControl: blocked or failed work is reported as complete

domains.name: Software Architecture
domains.purpose: Define durable module boundaries, responsibilities, interfaces, and tradeoffs before scale.
domains.subject: software structure and ownership boundaries
domains.artifact: architecture model and decision record
domains.evidence: dependency graph, critical flows, interface contracts, and failure-mode analysis
domains.invariant: responsibilities are explicit and dependencies do not violate ownership
domains.boundary: architecture decision versus local implementation detail
domains.risk: coupling, duplication, and hidden authority make change unsafe and expensive
domains.failureMechanism: code structure emerges from local edits without a system model
domains.recoveryAction: map data, flow, boundaries, and failure modes before choosing the smallest structural correction
domains.decisionAxis: local design, abstraction, module boundary, or architectural change
domains.mentalMechanism: architecture constrains the cost and safety of future change
domains.governanceControl: a durable boundary changes without a reviewed decision
positiveSignals: Cross-module coupling or durable interface change
negativeSignals: Existing owner and interface remain unchanged

domains.name: Systems Engineering
domains.purpose: Optimize end-to-end lifecycle outcomes across components, humans, operations, and retirement.
domains.subject: the complete sociotechnical system and lifecycle
domains.artifact: system context, interfaces, lifecycle, and tradeoff model
domains.evidence: end-to-end scenarios, interface evidence, operational constraints, and lifecycle risks
domains.invariant: local improvements do not degrade the whole-system outcome
domains.boundary: system integration versus component ownership
domains.risk: component success hides system failure, unsafe handoffs, or lifecycle debt
domains.failureMechanism: optimization ignores interactions, operations, users, or retirement costs
domains.recoveryAction: reconstruct the end-to-end system and evaluate cross-boundary effects
domains.decisionAxis: component repair, integration change, operational control, or system redesign
domains.mentalMechanism: emergent behavior arises from interactions and feedback across the lifecycle
domains.governanceControl: system-level risk lacks an accountable integrator

domains.name: Codebase Navigation
domains.purpose: Find the owning implementation, callers, tests, history, and constraints before editing.
domains.subject: repository structure and semantic ownership
domains.artifact: candidate path and dependency map
domains.evidence: current definitions, usages, neighboring tests, and applicable instructions
domains.invariant: changes land in the code that actually controls behavior
domains.boundary: derived search index versus authoritative current source
domains.risk: the wrong file is edited, logic is duplicated, or hidden callers regress
domains.failureMechanism: surface matches are mistaken for ownership without tracing control flow
domains.recoveryAction: step to the nearest code that computes or mutates the behavior
domains.decisionAxis: read locally, trace references, inspect history, or escalate discovery
domains.mentalMechanism: semantic ownership is discovered through evidence paths, not file names alone
domains.governanceControl: derived indexes override current source or repository instructions

domains.name: Implementation
domains.purpose: Translate an accepted design into the smallest readable, testable, maintainable change.
domains.subject: bounded source implementation
domains.artifact: owned code and configuration slice
domains.evidence: focused compile, tests, static analysis, and behavior observation
domains.invariant: the change solves the verified cause without unrelated mutation
domains.boundary: accepted implementation scope and source ownership
domains.risk: large speculative edits create regressions and obscure causality
domains.failureMechanism: implementation outruns understanding or combines multiple hypotheses
domains.recoveryAction: reduce to one owned slice and one falsifiable change
domains.decisionAxis: edit, probe, refactor locally, or stop
domains.mentalMechanism: small reversible changes preserve causal feedback and reviewability
domains.governanceControl: implementation begins before Definition of Ready or outside authority
positiveSignals: BUILD decision with ready acceptance criteria
negativeSignals: STOP, DEFER, or unresolved TEST decision

domains.name: Refactoring
domains.purpose: Improve internal structure while preserving externally observable behavior.
domains.subject: code structure under a behavior-preservation contract
domains.artifact: refactoring scope and regression suite
domains.evidence: before-and-after behavior tests, dependency checks, and diff review
domains.invariant: public behavior and data contracts remain unchanged unless separately approved
domains.boundary: structural improvement versus feature or migration work
domains.risk: a cleanup silently changes behavior, data, performance, or ownership
domains.failureMechanism: refactoring mixes functional changes or lacks characterization evidence
domains.recoveryAction: separate behavior change, add characterization tests, and reduce the structural step
domains.decisionAxis: leave, local cleanup, extract, redesign, or defer
domains.mentalMechanism: structure affects future change cost but preservation evidence constrains safe movement
domains.governanceControl: unrequested tidying expands scope or changes public contracts
positiveSignals: Duplication or coupling blocks accepted work

domains.name: Debugging And Root Cause Analysis
domains.purpose: Reproduce, classify, isolate, explain, and repair the first causal failure rather than its final symptom.
domains.subject: a reproducible defect or failed verifier
domains.artifact: failure timeline, hypothesis ledger, and minimal reproduction
domains.evidence: reproduction, logs, stack trace, history, environment, and focused regression
domains.invariant: each edit tests one changed hypothesis about the verified cause
domains.boundary: investigation evidence versus repair authority
domains.risk: random fixes mask the root cause and create cascading defects
domains.failureMechanism: the visible error is patched before the causal chain is understood
domains.recoveryAction: freeze edits, reconstruct the timeline, and test one new hypothesis
domains.decisionAxis: investigate, isolate, repair, rollback, or escalate
domains.mentalMechanism: causal diagnosis improves when hypotheses are falsifiable and evidence is time-ordered
domains.governanceControl: three failed hypotheses continue without a mandatory reset
positiveSignals: Failing test, crash, or incorrect behavior

domains.name: Testing
domains.purpose: Design layered checks that falsify defects and protect important behavior over time.
domains.subject: test strategy, fixtures, and coverage boundaries
domains.artifact: test matrix and executable suites
domains.evidence: failing-before, passing-after, edge, integration, and regression results
domains.invariant: tests exercise meaningful contracts rather than merely implementation details
domains.boundary: test evidence versus production observation
domains.risk: passing tests create false confidence while critical journeys remain untested
domains.failureMechanism: tests mirror the implementation, omit failure states, or use unrealistic fixtures
domains.recoveryAction: map risks to the cheapest reliable test level and add the missing falsifier
domains.decisionAxis: unit, integration, device, end-to-end, manual, or production observation
domains.mentalMechanism: test value depends on defect detection power, realism, speed, and maintenance cost
domains.governanceControl: sensitive or fabricated production data enters test fixtures

domains.name: Verification And Completion
domains.purpose: Require reproducible evidence before upgrading a claim from implemented to complete.
domains.subject: requirement satisfaction and completion claims
domains.artifact: requirement-to-evidence matrix and completion audit
domains.evidence: compile, tests, static analysis, runtime journeys, artifacts, and residual-risk review
domains.invariant: claim strength never exceeds the strongest relevant evidence
domains.boundary: implementation self-review versus independent acceptance
domains.risk: unfinished or unsafe work is released because code exists or one check passed
domains.failureMechanism: evidence gaps are hidden behind broad done language
domains.recoveryAction: downgrade the claim, enumerate missing evidence, and run the highest-value remaining check
domains.decisionAxis: not ready, conditionally ready, ready, or blocked
domains.mentalMechanism: confidence should update according to independent evidence quality, not effort spent
domains.governanceControl: the implementer waives a failed gate or certifies material risk alone
positiveSignals: Material change has executable acceptance criteria

domains.name: Release Engineering
domains.purpose: Produce traceable, reproducible, correctly identified artifacts and gate publication separately.
domains.subject: release candidate, artifacts, and distribution contract
domains.artifact: release manifest, signed artifacts, checksums, and release runbook
domains.evidence: build, lint, package identity, signature, alignment, upgrade, rollback, and channel checks
domains.invariant: the published artifact matches reviewed source, identity, permissions, and release decision
domains.boundary: artifact preparation versus publication authority
domains.risk: wrong, unsigned, over-permissioned, or unverified artifacts reach users
domains.failureMechanism: packaging success is mistaken for release readiness or publication approval
domains.recoveryAction: quarantine the candidate, reproduce artifacts, and rerun release gates
domains.decisionAxis: prepare, hold, rollback, stage, or publish with approval
domains.mentalMechanism: release quality is an end-to-end chain whose weakest artifact or approval invalidates the candidate
domains.governanceControl: tagging, publishing, signing, or deployment occurs without current approval
positiveSignals: Version, signing, or package identity work
negativeSignals: No artifact or public channel involved

domains.name: DevOps And Operations
domains.purpose: Operate build, deployment, configuration, and service workflows safely and repeatably.
domains.subject: operational environment and delivery automation
domains.artifact: runbook, pipeline, infrastructure configuration, and operational evidence
domains.evidence: dry runs, policy checks, deployment plans, health signals, and rollback verification
domains.invariant: operational mutations are explicit, least-privilege, observable, and reversible or approved
domains.boundary: engineering preparation versus operations and deployment authority
domains.risk: automation causes outages, data loss, secret exposure, or uncontrolled spend
domains.failureMechanism: environment assumptions and side effects are hidden inside commands or pipelines
domains.recoveryAction: stop automation, contain impact, restore from a verified runbook, and escalate
domains.decisionAxis: dry run, stage, deploy, rollback, or block
domains.mentalMechanism: automation amplifies both correctness and mistakes across environments
domains.governanceControl: production mutation, secret use, or spending lacks approval and audit evidence
positiveSignals: Pipeline, infrastructure, deployment, or service operation

domains.name: Reliability Engineering
domains.purpose: Keep critical user outcomes correct and available across faults, restarts, retries, and degradation.
domains.subject: service reliability and failure recovery
domains.artifact: reliability model, SLO evidence, fault tests, and recovery runbook
domains.evidence: failure injection, restart, retry, degradation, capacity, and recovery observations
domains.invariant: expected faults do not silently corrupt state or violate critical outcomes
domains.boundary: reliability engineering versus product risk acceptance
domains.risk: intermittent failures become data loss, prolonged outage, or false success
domains.failureMechanism: happy-path design omits fault containment, idempotency, or recovery evidence
domains.recoveryAction: contain the fault, restore the critical path, and add a regression or monitor
domains.decisionAxis: prevent, tolerate, degrade, recover, or accept with owner approval
domains.mentalMechanism: reliability emerges from fault isolation, redundancy, feedback, and bounded recovery
domains.governanceControl: critical reliability risk is accepted without an owner, SLO, or rollback
positiveSignals: Networked, concurrent, background, or persistent workflow

domains.name: Performance Engineering
domains.purpose: Measure and improve user-relevant latency, throughput, memory, energy, and capacity without guessing.
domains.subject: performance behavior and resource consumption
domains.artifact: benchmark, profile, budget, and capacity model
domains.evidence: repeatable measurements on representative workloads and environments
domains.invariant: optimization preserves correctness and targets a measured bottleneck
domains.boundary: performance recommendation versus product experience and infrastructure budget
domains.risk: premature or misdirected optimization adds complexity while real bottlenecks persist
domains.failureMechanism: anecdotes or synthetic microbenchmarks substitute for representative measurement
domains.recoveryAction: restore a baseline, profile the critical path, and optimize one measured constraint
domains.decisionAxis: measure, optimize, cache, scale, simplify, or defer
domains.mentalMechanism: queueing, locality, contention, and bottlenecks shape end-to-end performance
domains.governanceControl: performance claims lack reproducible methodology or environment context
positiveSignals: Latency, memory, battery, throughput, or capacity issue

domains.name: Security Engineering
domains.purpose: Protect assets by designing threat-aware boundaries, least privilege, and fail-closed controls.
domains.subject: security assets, threats, identities, and controls
domains.artifact: threat model, control design, test evidence, and residual-risk record
domains.evidence: abuse cases, authorization tests, secret scans, dependency checks, and control observations
domains.invariant: unauthorized actors cannot gain protected capability or data through expected threat paths
domains.boundary: security assurance versus accountable risk acceptance
domains.risk: confidentiality, integrity, availability, identity, or supply-chain compromise
domains.failureMechanism: trust is assumed at a boundary or a control exists only in UI or prompt text
domains.recoveryAction: contain exposure, revoke affected capability, preserve evidence, and repair the owning control
domains.decisionAxis: eliminate, prevent, detect, contain, transfer, or accept with authority
domains.mentalMechanism: attackers exploit the cheapest reachable boundary and compose small weaknesses
domains.governanceControl: security risk or exception is accepted without qualified ownership and audit
positiveSignals: Authentication, authorization, secrets, external input, supply chain, or sensitive data
negativeSignals: No asset or trust boundary changes
Pure public static content with no execution

domains.name: Privacy Engineering
domains.purpose: Minimize personal data and make collection, use, retention, access, and deletion proportionate and transparent.
domains.subject: personal data lifecycle and user expectations
domains.artifact: data inventory, flow map, retention/deletion design, and disclosure
domains.evidence: field-level inventory, access tests, lifecycle observations, and policy-to-code mapping
domains.invariant: personal data is collected and exposed only for an explicit necessary purpose
domains.boundary: product data use versus legal and privacy risk acceptance
domains.risk: unnecessary collection, unauthorized exposure, misleading disclosure, or irreversible retention
domains.failureMechanism: data enters a system without ownership, minimization, lifecycle, or user expectation review
domains.recoveryAction: stop collection or sharing, contain data, correct lifecycle controls, and update disclosure
domains.decisionAxis: avoid, minimize, localize, aggregate, retain, delete, or obtain approval
domains.mentalMechanism: privacy risk accumulates across collection, linkage, access, retention, and inference
domains.governanceControl: personal data processing lacks purpose, owner, retention, deletion, or disclosure
positiveSignals: Personal, behavioral, financial, health, identity, or device data
Analytics, sync, export, or sharing change

domains.name: Compliance And Legal Readiness
domains.purpose: Map applicable obligations to owned controls and qualified approval without inventing legal conclusions.
domains.subject: regulatory, contractual, policy, and licensing obligations
domains.artifact: obligation-control matrix and legal review record
domains.evidence: authoritative requirement, jurisdiction scope, control mapping, and qualified review
domains.invariant: the product does not claim compliance beyond reviewed and implemented controls
domains.boundary: engineering evidence versus qualified legal interpretation and acceptance
domains.risk: unlawful processing, contractual breach, licensing violation, or misleading public claims
domains.failureMechanism: generic guidance is applied as jurisdiction-specific approval
domains.recoveryAction: pause the affected release or processing, identify qualified ownership, and close the control gap
domains.decisionAxis: in scope, out of scope, seek counsel, implement control, or block release
domains.mentalMechanism: compliance depends on context, jurisdiction, evidence, and continuing operations rather than one checklist
domains.governanceControl: legal or compliance acceptance is performed by an unqualified agent
positiveSignals: Regulated domain, licensing, terms, policy, or jurisdiction question

domains.name: Data Engineering
domains.purpose: Design traceable ingestion, transformation, quality, lineage, and lifecycle for analytical or operational data.
domains.subject: data pipelines, schemas, lineage, and quality
domains.artifact: data contract, lineage map, validation suite, and pipeline run evidence
domains.evidence: source-to-output reconciliation, schema checks, quality metrics, and replay tests
domains.invariant: derived data is traceable to authoritative inputs and deterministic transformations
domains.boundary: data production versus downstream interpretation and business use
domains.risk: silent corruption, duplication, loss, stale conclusions, or ungoverned personal data
domains.failureMechanism: pipeline transformations lack contracts, idempotency, lineage, or reconciliation
domains.recoveryAction: stop propagation, identify the first bad transformation, replay from trusted input, and reconcile
domains.decisionAxis: reject, quarantine, transform, backfill, replay, or deprecate
domains.mentalMechanism: data quality degrades multiplicatively as errors propagate through dependent transformations
domains.governanceControl: untraceable or sensitive data is published without ownership and lifecycle controls
positiveSignals: ETL, analytics, event stream, import/export, or data migration

domains.name: Database Integrity
domains.purpose: Preserve ownership, constraints, transactions, migrations, and recoverability of persisted state.
domains.subject: transactional schema and persisted invariants
domains.artifact: schema, migration, DAO, transaction boundary, and integrity tests
domains.evidence: migration tests, constraint failures, transaction rollback, restart, and reconciliation results
domains.invariant: persisted state remains valid, attributable, and recoverable across versions and failures
domains.boundary: database authority versus cache, UI state, and external projections
domains.risk: data loss, wrong ownership, duplicate effects, partial commits, or unrecoverable upgrades
domains.failureMechanism: schema and mutation paths diverge from entity constraints or migration history
domains.recoveryAction: stop writes, reproduce on a copy, repair the smallest schema or transaction boundary, and validate upgrade paths
domains.decisionAxis: constraint, transaction, migration, repair, rebuild, or block
domains.mentalMechanism: persistent invariants must survive every write path, failure point, and version transition
domains.governanceControl: destructive migration or data rewrite lacks backup, rollback, and owner approval
positiveSignals: Schema, migration, persistence, cache, or transaction change

domains.name: API And Interface Contracts
domains.purpose: Make service and module interfaces explicit, bounded, versioned, and failure-aware.
domains.subject: API inputs, outputs, errors, compatibility, and ownership
domains.artifact: interface specification, schema, examples, and contract tests
domains.evidence: consumer/provider tests, malformed input tests, compatibility checks, and observed responses
domains.invariant: callers and providers share one enforceable contract across success and failure paths
domains.boundary: public interface versus internal implementation
domains.risk: silent incompatibility, unsafe input, ambiguous errors, or cascading client failures
domains.failureMechanism: implementation details become an undocumented contract or versioning is ignored
domains.recoveryAction: freeze incompatible rollout, restore the accepted contract, and add boundary validation
domains.decisionAxis: add, version, deprecate, adapt, reject, or remove
domains.mentalMechanism: interfaces concentrate coupling and propagate ambiguity across ownership boundaries
domains.governanceControl: breaking contract changes lack versioning, migration, consumer evidence, or approval
positiveSignals: Public method, endpoint, event, file format, or module interface change

domains.name: Distributed Systems
domains.purpose: Reason explicitly about partial failure, consistency, ordering, retries, identity, and network partitions.
domains.subject: multi-node state and networked coordination
domains.artifact: consistency model, protocol, idempotency design, and convergence tests
domains.evidence: partition, retry, duplicate, reordering, stale-read, and convergence observations
domains.invariant: authorized participants converge without double effects or silent overwrite under expected failures
domains.boundary: server authority, client cache, network, and external service ownership
domains.risk: split-brain state, duplicate money effects, stale authorization, or irreconcilable history
domains.failureMechanism: single-process assumptions are applied across unreliable networks and clocks
domains.recoveryAction: identify authoritative state, contain writes, reconcile using idempotent history, and expose staleness
domains.decisionAxis: strong consistency, eventual consistency, reject, retry, reconcile, or degrade
domains.mentalMechanism: networks permit delay, duplication, reordering, and partition rather than reliable immediate delivery
domains.governanceControl: distributed financial or authorization writes lack server authority and idempotency
positiveSignals: Synchronization, queues, services, multiple devices, or remote state
Retries or network failures affect correctness

domains.name: Frontend And User Experience
domains.purpose: Make critical user workflows understandable, efficient, responsive, and honest across states.
domains.subject: user interface, interaction flow, and visible system state
domains.artifact: screen flow, UI state model, copy, and interaction evidence
domains.evidence: runtime walkthroughs, screenshots, usability checks, and state coverage
domains.invariant: users can understand the primary action, current state, consequences, and recovery path
domains.boundary: presentation and interaction versus domain authority and persisted truth
domains.risk: users make wrong decisions, lose work, or trust misleading state
domains.failureMechanism: happy-path visuals hide loading, error, empty, stale, permission, or irreversible states
domains.recoveryAction: restore truthful state, simplify the critical path, and test realistic user journeys
domains.decisionAxis: show, hide, disable, explain, confirm, defer, or remove
domains.mentalMechanism: attention and feedback shape user decisions more than feature count
domains.governanceControl: UI copy promises security, certainty, or completion the implementation cannot enforce
positiveSignals: Screen, workflow, interaction, state, or user-facing copy change

domains.name: Accessibility
domains.purpose: Ensure people with diverse abilities can perceive, understand, navigate, and operate the product.
domains.subject: accessible semantics, interaction, content, and assistive technology behavior
domains.artifact: accessibility requirements, semantic tree, contrast/layout evidence, and device tests
domains.evidence: screen reader, keyboard, switch, scaling, contrast, target-size, and error-recovery checks
domains.invariant: critical functionality does not depend on one sensory, motor, or cognitive ability
domains.boundary: accessibility engineering versus qualified user research and legal review
domains.risk: users are excluded from essential workflows or receive inaccessible error and safety information
domains.failureMechanism: visual appearance is tested while semantics, focus, scaling, and alternative input are ignored
domains.recoveryAction: identify the blocked journey, repair semantics or interaction, and validate with assistive technology
domains.decisionAxis: adapt, simplify, annotate, reorder, provide alternative, or block release
domains.mentalMechanism: disability emerges from interaction between user capability, environment, and design barriers
domains.governanceControl: critical accessibility failures are waived without accountable approval and evidence
positiveSignals: UI, content, navigation, input, animation, or error-state change

domains.name: Mobile Engineering
domains.purpose: Design for device lifecycle, permissions, offline behavior, battery, storage, upgrades, and platform variability.
domains.subject: mobile application lifecycle and device integration
domains.artifact: mobile state model, manifests, device matrix, and lifecycle tests
domains.evidence: device/emulator runs, process death, restart, permission, network, background, upgrade, and resource observations
domains.invariant: critical state remains correct across mobile lifecycle transitions and constrained devices
domains.boundary: application behavior versus operating system, hardware, store, and vendor policies
domains.risk: background work stops, state is lost, permissions mislead, or behavior differs across devices
domains.failureMechanism: desktop or foreground assumptions ignore process death, vendor limits, and platform lifecycle
domains.recoveryAction: reproduce on the target device state, persist authoritative state, and add lifecycle evidence
domains.decisionAxis: persist, defer, schedule, degrade, request permission, or use platform service
domains.mentalMechanism: mobile platforms reclaim resources and mediate capabilities according to lifecycle and policy
domains.governanceControl: permissions, background behavior, or store claims exceed implemented need and disclosure
positiveSignals: Device lifecycle, permission, background, or store change

domains.name: Environment And Hardware Awareness
domains.purpose: Adapt engineering strategy to actual OS, hardware, storage, network, tools, and developer capacity.
domains.subject: execution environment and resource constraints
domains.artifact: environment snapshot and resource-aware execution plan
domains.evidence: measured RAM, disk, process, SDK, device, network, and tool availability
domains.invariant: the plan fits the real environment without avoidable failure or resource exhaustion
domains.boundary: observed local environment versus assumed or recommended infrastructure
domains.risk: builds hang, storage is exhausted, downloads are blocked, or user devices are modified unexpectedly
domains.failureMechanism: generic instructions ignore measured hardware, network, policy, and connected-device state
domains.recoveryAction: stop overlapping workloads, measure constraints, and choose a lower-resource path
domains.decisionAxis: local tool, emulator, physical device, remote runner, defer, or reduce scope
domains.mentalMechanism: available resources constrain feasible workflows and change the optimal verification strategy
domains.governanceControl: tools install, download, elevate, or touch devices without need and approval
positiveSignals: Build, emulator, device, storage, network, or installation work

domains.name: AI Agent Engineering
domains.purpose: Design bounded agents that reason, use tools, collaborate, stop, and report evidence predictably.
domains.subject: agent goals, capabilities, loops, and lifecycle
domains.artifact: agent contract, tool policy, checkpoints, and evaluation suite
domains.evidence: task traces, tool outcomes, stop-state tests, adversarial fixtures, and human correction rates
domains.invariant: agent autonomy remains bounded, observable, reversible, and aligned with the mission
domains.boundary: model reasoning versus deterministic runtime, authority, and human approval
domains.risk: agents drift, fabricate success, misuse tools, consume resources, or exceed authority
domains.failureMechanism: model fluency is treated as control, memory, verification, or governance
domains.recoveryAction: stop the loop, restore the contract, inspect the first failed step, and reroute
domains.decisionAxis: deterministic tool, single agent, specialist, multi-agent, human gate, or no AI
domains.mentalMechanism: reliable agency emerges from constrained feedback loops around an uncertain model
domains.governanceControl: agent prompts grant authority or bypass deterministic enforcement
positiveSignals: Agent, prompt runtime, tool use, autonomous loop, or multi-agent design

domains.name: Model Routing
domains.purpose: Select the least costly capable model or deterministic tool for each bounded reasoning need.
domains.subject: model and non-model execution choice
domains.artifact: routing policy, capability registry, budget, and outcome telemetry
domains.evidence: quality, latency, cost, privacy, context, and failure measurements by task class
domains.invariant: expensive or external models are used only when they add measurable decision value
domains.boundary: routing recommendation versus configured model availability and budget ownership
domains.risk: token cost, latency, privacy exposure, or weak-model failure grows without outcome benefit
domains.failureMechanism: one model is used for every task or routing follows brand assumptions instead of evidence
domains.recoveryAction: classify the task, compare measured capability, and downgrade or escalate with a budget
domains.decisionAxis: deterministic tool, cache, local model, small cloud model, strong model, or human
domains.mentalMechanism: routing is an optimization under quality, cost, latency, privacy, and risk constraints
domains.governanceControl: unapproved model, data transfer, or budget expansion occurs through routing
positiveSignals: Token, latency, privacy, or capability constraint

domains.name: Context Engineering
domains.purpose: Provide sufficient, relevant, fresh, isolated, economical, and provenance-bearing context to reasoning systems.
domains.subject: model context selection and freshness
domains.artifact: context pack, routing trace, provenance, and invalidation record
domains.evidence: retrieval relevance, source verification, token size, freshness, and task outcomes
domains.invariant: derived context routes back to authoritative current evidence and excludes unnecessary sensitive data
domains.boundary: authoritative source versus memory, summary, index, and untrusted retrieved text
domains.risk: context bloat, prompt injection, stale decisions, privacy leakage, and architecture amnesia
domains.failureMechanism: large or convenient context replaces selective verified evidence
domains.recoveryAction: invalidate stale context, rebuild from authoritative sources, and enforce a budget
domains.decisionAxis: include, summarize, retrieve, isolate, expire, or exclude
domains.mentalMechanism: reasoning quality depends on relevance and provenance, not maximum context volume
domains.governanceControl: untrusted text becomes instruction or sensitive context is persisted without need
positiveSignals: Long session, large repository, retrieval, memory, or prompt construction

domains.name: Memory And Knowledge Management
domains.purpose: Preserve durable verified decisions and lessons while expiring stale or sensitive derived knowledge.
domains.subject: project, procedural, episodic, and user-preference memory
domains.artifact: versioned memory record and knowledge index
domains.evidence: provenance, freshness, owner, invalidation, source revision, and retrieval outcomes
domains.invariant: memory accelerates routing but never overrides stronger current evidence
domains.boundary: source of truth versus durable memory and rebuildable indexes
domains.risk: stale facts, repeated mistakes, privacy leakage, and contradictory decisions persist across sessions
domains.failureMechanism: chat history or generated summaries are treated as authoritative project state
domains.recoveryAction: mark the fact stale, inspect authoritative sources, and update or remove memory
domains.decisionAxis: store, update, expire, delete, index, or keep session-only
domains.mentalMechanism: memory is useful when selective, consolidated, retrievable, and continuously invalidated
domains.governanceControl: secrets, private content, or unsupported inference enters persistent memory
positiveSignals: Durable decision, recurring mistake, architecture fact, or cross-session preference

domains.name: Prompt And Specification Engineering
domains.purpose: Encode focused task contracts and machine-readable rules without burying authority or context.
domains.subject: prompts, instructions, schemas, and behavior specifications
domains.artifact: versioned customization or specification file
domains.evidence: frontmatter/schema validation, discovery tests, negative fixtures, and task outcomes
domains.invariant: instructions are scoped, discoverable, non-conflicting, and subordinate to system authority
domains.boundary: guidance in prompts versus deterministic enforcement and runtime policy
domains.risk: rules are ignored, conflict, enable injection, bloat context, or imply unsupported capability
domains.failureMechanism: one giant prompt mixes concerns and assumes prose can enforce permissions
domains.recoveryAction: split by concern, tighten discovery, move enforcement into deterministic validation, and test activation
domains.decisionAxis: instruction, prompt, skill, agent, hook, schema, or no customization
domains.mentalMechanism: specification quality depends on scope, discoverability, precedence, and executable feedback
domains.governanceControl: prompt text grants tools, secrets, authority, or destructive behavior
positiveSignals: Create or modify AI instructions, prompts, agents, skills, hooks, or schemas

domains.name: Tool Orchestration
domains.purpose: Choose, parameterize, sequence, observe, and stop tools safely according to their real capabilities.
domains.subject: tool selection and execution workflow
domains.artifact: tool plan, parameter contract, and observed outputs
domains.evidence: tool definitions, validated parameters, exit states, outputs, and side-effect checks
domains.invariant: tools operate only within declared scope and every result is inspected before the next mutation
domains.boundary: model intent versus tool capability and external side effects
domains.risk: wrong parameters, hidden prompts, destructive actions, leaked secrets, or false success
domains.failureMechanism: the model invents tool behavior or chains actions without observing intermediate results
domains.recoveryAction: stop the chain, inspect tool contracts and current state, then rerun one bounded action
domains.decisionAxis: read tool, edit tool, terminal, browser, subagent, ask human, or no tool
domains.mentalMechanism: tool reliability depends on explicit contracts, observability, sequencing, and side-effect control
domains.governanceControl: a tool performs destructive, external, privileged, or sensitive action without approval

domains.name: Cost And Engineering Economics
domains.purpose: Optimize total outcome cost across tokens, developer time, cloud, maintenance, support, and opportunity cost.
domains.subject: engineering resource allocation and recurring cost
domains.artifact: cost model, budget, routing policy, and measured usage
domains.evidence: provider invoices, token logs, build time, support load, infrastructure metrics, and owner constraints
domains.invariant: cost claims use measured inputs and optimization does not sacrifice critical quality or safety
domains.boundary: engineering estimate versus financial commitment and budget approval
domains.risk: tokenmaxxing, cloud overrun, hidden maintenance burden, and low-value work consume runway
domains.failureMechanism: unit prices or free tiers are treated as total cost without workload and lifecycle measurement
domains.recoveryAction: stop unbounded spend, measure cost drivers, route cheaper work, cache, and cut low-value scope
domains.decisionAxis: avoid, cache, route, optimize, buy, build, defer, or stop
domains.mentalMechanism: total cost includes direct spend, delay, maintenance, risk, and opportunity cost
domains.governanceControl: spending, quotas, or financial commitments expand without owner approval and alerts
positiveSignals: Token, cloud, staffing, build, support, or vendor cost decision

domains.name: Observability
domains.purpose: Make system state, failures, latency, cost, and agent behavior diagnosable without exposing sensitive content.
domains.subject: logs, metrics, traces, events, and health signals
domains.artifact: observability schema, dashboards, alerts, and diagnostic runbook
domains.evidence: signal coverage, redaction tests, alert exercises, trace correlation, and incident use
domains.invariant: critical failures are detectable and attributable without leaking protected data
domains.boundary: operational telemetry versus user content, secrets, and privacy
domains.risk: silent failures persist, incidents take longer, or sensitive data enters logs
domains.failureMechanism: systems emit raw volume without actionable correlation, ownership, or retention
domains.recoveryAction: add the minimum diagnostic signal at the first uncertain boundary and verify alert-to-action flow
domains.decisionAxis: log, metric, trace, event, sample, redact, alert, or omit
domains.mentalMechanism: observability reduces uncertainty by connecting state transitions across time and boundaries
domains.governanceControl: sensitive payloads, credentials, or unbounded telemetry are collected without need and retention
positiveSignals: Runtime service, agent, pipeline, cost, or incident work

domains.name: Incident Response
domains.purpose: Detect, contain, communicate, recover, and learn from active production or data-integrity harm.
domains.subject: active incident and affected users or systems
domains.artifact: incident timeline, containment record, runbook, and postmortem
domains.evidence: alerts, logs, impact scope, state snapshots, recovery checks, and communications
domains.invariant: containment protects people and data while preserving evidence and authority
domains.boundary: incident coordination versus source repair, legal, security, and public communication ownership
domains.risk: continued harm, evidence loss, unsafe recovery, misinformation, or recurrence
domains.failureMechanism: repair begins before impact, authority, containment, and evidence are understood
domains.recoveryAction: activate incident command, contain impact, preserve evidence, restore safely, and run a blameless review
domains.decisionAxis: observe, contain, rollback, disable, recover, communicate, or escalate
domains.mentalMechanism: incident outcomes depend on response latency, coordination, containment, and learning loops
domains.governanceControl: production action or public communication occurs outside incident authority
positiveSignals: Active outage, breach, data corruption, or critical user harm

domains.name: Risk And Safety Engineering
domains.purpose: Identify hazards, controls, failure costs, and safe states before high-impact work proceeds.
domains.subject: hazards, harms, controls, and residual risk
domains.artifact: risk register, hazard analysis, safety case, and control evidence
domains.evidence: scenarios, probability/impact basis, control tests, independent review, and owner acceptance
domains.invariant: unacceptable harm is prevented or the activity remains blocked
domains.boundary: engineering risk analysis versus qualified and accountable risk acceptance
domains.risk: physical, financial, operational, legal, or social harm exceeds tolerable limits
domains.failureMechanism: low probability, hidden coupling, or human factors are ignored until after failure
domains.recoveryAction: move to a safe state, contain exposure, strengthen controls, and seek qualified review
domains.decisionAxis: eliminate, reduce, detect, contain, transfer, accept, or stop
domains.mentalMechanism: risk combines likelihood, exposure, impact, detectability, and control dependence
domains.governanceControl: material safety risk is self-approved by the implementer or hidden in aggregate scoring
positiveSignals: High-impact data, finance, health, safety, security, or irreversible action

domains.name: AI And Engineering Governance
domains.purpose: Define authority, approval, audit, exceptions, lifecycle, and accountability outside model prompts.
domains.subject: engineering authority and policy enforcement
domains.artifact: governance policy, approval record, audit trail, and exception register
domains.evidence: enforcement tests, authority mapping, approvals, logs, and periodic review
domains.invariant: no agent or contributor can grant itself authority or waive a failed gate
domains.boundary: policy definition, enforcement, exception approval, and implementation roles
domains.risk: unlimited autonomy, unaudited changes, unsafe exceptions, and accountability gaps
domains.failureMechanism: governance exists as prose without deterministic controls or named owners
domains.recoveryAction: stop unauthorized work, restore the policy boundary, audit effects, and route exceptions
domains.decisionAxis: allow, constrain, require approval, audit, suspend, deprecate, or prohibit
domains.mentalMechanism: governance shapes incentives and constrains power through enforceable institutions
domains.governanceControl: the same actor proposes, implements, verifies, and accepts material risk without separation
positiveSignals: Authority, approval, policy, audit, agent lifecycle, or exception change
negativeSignals: Low-risk action already inside accepted authority

domains.name: Documentation Engineering
domains.purpose: Preserve accurate, navigable, audience-specific knowledge that remains useful after the conversation.
domains.subject: technical, product, operational, and decision documentation
domains.artifact: versioned documentation set and link graph
domains.evidence: source comparison, runnable examples, resolved links, audience review, and freshness checks
domains.invariant: documentation describes verified current reality and routes readers to authoritative sources
domains.boundary: normative source versus explanation, generated index, and historical record
domains.risk: operators, users, and future engineers act on stale or misleading guidance
domains.failureMechanism: documentation is updated from memory, duplicates source, or mixes current and historical claims
domains.recoveryAction: compare against current source and evidence, correct the owning document, and invalidate stale copies
domains.decisionAxis: document, link, generate, archive, deprecate, or omit
domains.mentalMechanism: documentation is an interface whose usefulness depends on accuracy, structure, audience, and maintenance
domains.governanceControl: generated or historical text is presented as current normative policy
positiveSignals: Behavior, architecture, operation, release, or decision changes
negativeSignals: Transient experiment with no durable consequence

domains.name: Human-AI Collaboration
domains.purpose: Build shared understanding, constructive challenge, calibrated trust, and adaptive working relationships.
domains.subject: human-agent collaboration over a mission
domains.artifact: shared mental model, decision packet, and progress evidence
domains.evidence: clarification quality, correction rate, accepted decisions, handoffs, and user outcomes
domains.invariant: the human remains informed, able to contest decisions, and in control of consequential actions
domains.boundary: adaptive collaboration versus simulated emotion, manipulation, and authority
domains.risk: false agreement, frustration, misunderstanding, dependency, or loss of trust
domains.failureMechanism: request-response generation replaces joint investigation and explicit decisions
domains.recoveryAction: pause output, restate shared understanding, expose disagreement, and choose a collaboration mode
domains.decisionAxis: ask, listen, challenge, explain, act, wait, summarize, or escalate
domains.mentalMechanism: trust grows from competence, honesty, predictability, shared control, and repair after error
domains.governanceControl: the agent manipulates emotion, hides uncertainty, or bypasses human approval
positiveSignals: Ambiguity, correction, frustration, learning, or consequential decision

domains.name: Team Coordination
domains.purpose: Coordinate owners, dependencies, independent review, conflicts, and handoffs across a team.
domains.subject: multi-person or multi-agent work allocation and integration
domains.artifact: assignment contracts, dependency map, handoffs, and integration record
domains.evidence: ownership, isolated scopes, review results, conflict decisions, and merged outcomes
domains.invariant: parallel work has one integration owner and does not silently overlap authority or write scope
domains.boundary: coordination authority versus specialist and source-owner decisions
domains.risk: duplicated work, conflicting edits, groupthink, dropped handoffs, and unclear accountability
domains.failureMechanism: roles are assigned by title without capability, authority, inputs, outputs, and stop conditions
domains.recoveryAction: stop overlapping work, restate assignments, isolate scopes, and route conflicts to owners
domains.decisionAxis: delegate, pair, parallelize, sequence, review, integrate, or escalate
domains.mentalMechanism: team performance depends on coordination cost, shared models, specialization, and conflict quality
domains.governanceControl: multiple actors mutate the same artifact without isolated ownership and integration control

domains.name: Technical Communication
domains.purpose: Exchange decisions, evidence, uncertainty, and action clearly for the intended audience.
domains.subject: technical and product communication
domains.artifact: decision packet, status update, explanation, or incident message
domains.evidence: audience comprehension, accurate references, action clarity, and feedback
domains.invariant: communication is truthful, proportionate, actionable, and free of fabricated certainty
domains.boundary: informing and recommending versus deciding or persuading through manipulation
domains.risk: misunderstanding, hidden risk, wasted effort, and damaged trust
domains.failureMechanism: language optimizes fluency or agreement instead of shared meaning and evidence
domains.recoveryAction: simplify, restate decisions and unknowns, verify understanding, and correct the record
domains.decisionAxis: inform, ask, challenge, recommend, teach, warn, summarize, or remain silent
domains.mentalMechanism: communication succeeds when sender and receiver converge on meaning, not when text is merely delivered
domains.governanceControl: public, legal, security, or incident claims exceed evidence or authority
positiveSignals: Decision, handoff, explanation, warning, or progress report

domains.name: Learning And Teaching
domains.purpose: Develop durable understanding and expertise through evidence, practice, feedback, and reflection.
domains.subject: human or agent learning objective and skill development
domains.artifact: learning plan, worked examples, practice evidence, and reflection record
domains.evidence: retrieval, transfer, error patterns, confidence calibration, and independent performance
domains.invariant: teaching increases the learner's capability rather than only producing an answer
domains.boundary: educational support versus diagnosis, grading authority, and doing all work for the learner
domains.risk: shallow imitation, repeated mistakes, overload, dependency, or false confidence
domains.failureMechanism: explanation volume substitutes for active practice and feedback
domains.recoveryAction: reduce the concept, observe an attempt, give one targeted cue, and retest transfer
domains.decisionAxis: explain, demonstrate, scaffold, practice, retrieve, reflect, or pause
domains.mentalMechanism: learning strengthens through retrieval, spacing, feedback, varied practice, and manageable cognitive load
domains.governanceControl: the system diagnoses ability or health, shames mistakes, or claims learning without performance evidence
positiveSignals: Teaching, onboarding, skill development, or repeated mistake
User asks to understand rather than only receive output

domains.name: Cognitive And Human Factors
domains.purpose: Adapt systems and workflows to attention, memory, fatigue, cognitive load, bias, and real human capability.
domains.subject: human cognition and performance within engineering work
domains.artifact: human-factors analysis, workload model, and adapted workflow
domains.evidence: observable errors, task load, timing, usability, fatigue signals, and user feedback
domains.invariant: the workflow does not rely on unrealistic attention, memory, speed, or emotional state
domains.boundary: observable collaboration adaptation versus medical or psychological diagnosis
domains.risk: overload, tunnel vision, mistakes, exclusion, burnout, and unsafe decisions
domains.failureMechanism: the plan assumes unlimited attention, energy, memory, or expertise
domains.recoveryAction: reduce scope, externalize state, add checkpoints, change modality, and retest performance
domains.decisionAxis: simplify, pause, externalize, automate, teach, confirm, or escalate
domains.mentalMechanism: human performance varies with workload, environment, expertise, stress, feedback, and interface design
domains.governanceControl: the agent infers sensitive traits or manipulates users instead of responding to observable task signals
positiveSignals: Long session, repeated errors, confusion, overload, or safety-critical human action

domains.name: Decision Science
domains.purpose: Make explicit choices under uncertainty using evidence, alternatives, value, risk, and reversibility.
domains.subject: material engineering or product decision
domains.artifact: decision record with evidence ledger and falsifier
domains.evidence: option comparison, probability basis, costs, harms, owner values, and post-decision outcomes
domains.invariant: the chosen option follows an explicit rule and can be revised when evidence changes
domains.boundary: decision analysis versus accountable value and risk acceptance
domains.risk: bias, anchoring, sunk cost, fake precision, and hidden tradeoffs drive irreversible choices
domains.failureMechanism: the preferred answer is selected before alternatives and falsifiers are examined
domains.recoveryAction: reframe one decision question, label evidence, compare no-build, and run the cheapest falsifier
domains.decisionAxis: stop, defer, test, build, choose, or revisit
domains.mentalMechanism: good decisions maximize expected value under uncertainty while respecting constraints and option value
domains.governanceControl: the analyst invents probabilities, deadlines, economics, or owner preferences
positiveSignals: Material uncertainty, tradeoff, or irreversible consequence
negativeSignals: Deterministic requirement with one compliant implementation

domains.name: Ethics And Trust Engineering
domains.purpose: Design systems and collaboration that are honest, contestable, fair, accountable, and worthy of reliance.
domains.subject: ethical impact, trust claims, and affected stakeholders
domains.artifact: stakeholder analysis, limitation disclosure, contestability path, and trust evidence
domains.evidence: harm scenarios, affected-party input, control observations, auditability, and correction outcomes
domains.invariant: people are not deceived, coerced, unfairly burdened, or denied meaningful recourse
domains.boundary: engineering ethical analysis versus legal, cultural, and accountable value judgments
domains.risk: manipulation, discrimination, exclusion, overreliance, hidden harm, and unjustified trust
domains.failureMechanism: capability and convenience are optimized without affected-party agency, transparency, or recourse
domains.recoveryAction: stop the harmful path, disclose limits, restore contestability, and involve accountable stakeholders
domains.decisionAxis: avoid, redesign, disclose, obtain consent, add recourse, limit, or stop
domains.mentalMechanism: trust is earned through aligned incentives, competence, honesty, predictability, and accountability
domains.governanceControl: the system pretends emotion, authority, certainty, fairness, or safety it cannot substantiate
positiveSignals: Consequential automation, persuasion, ranking, surveillance, or vulnerable users
negativeSignals: No affected stakeholder beyond the operator

collaborationScopes.name: Product And Engineering
collaborationScopes.purpose: Align user outcome with feasible implementation.
collaborationScopes.decisionOwner: Product owner for outcome; engineering owner for implementation

collaborationScopes.name: Engineering And Architecture
collaborationScopes.purpose: Align local changes with durable boundaries.
collaborationScopes.challengeOwner: Source owner with current code evidence

collaborationScopes.name: Engineering And Security
collaborationScopes.purpose: Integrate controls without hiding product or operational cost.
collaborationScopes.decisionOwner: Engineering owner within non-waivable security constraints

collaborationScopes.name: Engineering And Privacy
collaborationScopes.purpose: Minimize data and map lifecycle controls.

collaborationScopes.name: Engineering And Data
collaborationScopes.purpose: Preserve schema, lineage, and transactional authority.

collaborationScopes.name: Engineering And Quality
collaborationScopes.purpose: Convert requirements into falsifiable acceptance evidence.

collaborationScopes.name: Engineering And Operations
collaborationScopes.purpose: Make services deployable, observable, and recoverable.

collaborationScopes.name: Engineering And Release
collaborationScopes.purpose: Separate artifact readiness from publication authority.

collaborationScopes.name: Engineering And Design
collaborationScopes.purpose: Align interaction intent with feasible truthful states.

collaborationScopes.name: Engineering And Accessibility
collaborationScopes.purpose: Integrate assistive behavior into critical flows.

collaborationScopes.name: Engineering And Performance
collaborationScopes.purpose: Target measured bottlenecks without weakening correctness.

collaborationScopes.name: Engineering And Support
collaborationScopes.purpose: Turn real user failures into reproducible, prioritized work.

collaborationScopes.name: Engineering And Legal
collaborationScopes.purpose: Map technical controls to qualified obligations.
collaborationScopes.decisionOwner: Qualified legal owner for legal acceptance

collaborationScopes.name: Engineering And Finance
collaborationScopes.purpose: Align architecture with budget and cost controls.

collaborationScopes.name: Founder And Engineering
collaborationScopes.purpose: Protect product focus while preserving technical integrity.
collaborationScopes.challengeOwner: Engineering lead for feasibility and risk

collaborationScopes.name: Manager And Engineer
collaborationScopes.purpose: Align outcome, capacity, ownership, and support.

collaborationScopes.name: Senior And Junior Engineer
collaborationScopes.purpose: Transfer capability while protecting production quality.

collaborationScopes.name: Developer And AI Agent
collaborationScopes.purpose: Keep the human informed and in control while using agent capability.
collaborationScopes.artifact: mission, plan, changes, and evidence record
collaborationScopes.challengeOwner: AI agent for evidence-based technical challenge

collaborationScopes.name: Human Approval Gate
collaborationScopes.purpose: Separate AI preparation from irreversible human approval.

collaborationScopes.name: Incident Command Team
collaborationScopes.purpose: Coordinate containment, evidence, recovery, and communication.

collaborationScopes.name: Cross-Team Handoff
collaborationScopes.purpose: Transfer ownership without hidden context or dropped obligations.

collaborationScopes.name: Vendor Integration
collaborationScopes.purpose: Align external capability, contracts, failure modes, and exit strategy.
collaborationScopes.challengeOwner: Independent security, legal, or architecture reviewer

collaborationScopes.name: Open Source Contribution
collaborationScopes.purpose: Integrate external changes under repository standards and licensing.

collaborationScopes.name: Research And Engineering
collaborationScopes.purpose: Translate uncertain findings into bounded reproducible implementation decisions.

collaborationScopes.name: Customer Feedback Loop
collaborationScopes.purpose: Convert feedback into evidence without promising unapproved scope.
collaborationScopes.challengeOwner: Engineering owner for feasibility and evidence

goldenCases.reason: A reproduced runtime defect activates diagnosis and reliability behavior.
